channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: South Africa Aligns with Namibia, Zimbabwe, Zambia and Mozambique to Welcome August Safari Travellers as Hidden Malaria Risks Persist: What Should Every Visitor Know Now Before Exploring Southern Africa?
item.description: South Africa Aligns with Namibia, Zimbabwe, Zambia and Mozambique to Welcome August Safari Travellers as Hidden Malaria Risks Persist: What Should Every Visitor Know Now Before Exploring Southern Africa? — via Travel And Tour World #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Travel And Tour World

item.title: FROM WASTE TO WEALTH: Dr. Dilesh Nguwaya Appointed Presidential Special Advisor on Waste Management and Environmental Sustainability, Writes Masimba Mavaza
item.description: By Dr Masimba Mavaza President Dr. Emmerson Mnangagwa has made a bold, future-facing appointment.Dr. Dilesh Nguwaya, Executive Chairperson and Chief... Continue reading "FROM WASTE TO WEALTH: Dr. Dilesh Nguwaya Appointed Presidential Special Advisor on Waste Management and Environmental Sustainability, Writes Masimba Mavaza" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Nothabo Zandile Tshuma’s mother bares all after her daughter’s husband was arrested in SA just before committing suicide
item.description: JOHANNESBURG – The anguish of a mother, Velile Khumalo, 76, has come to the forefront following the dramatic arrest of Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, in Johannesburg, South Africa. Tshuma, the husband of Nothabo Zandile Tshuma, 42, and father to their two young daughters, Natalie, 15, and Nala, 5, is the prime suspect in their brutal [...] The post Nothabo Zandile Tshuma’s mother bares all after her daughter’s husband was arrested in SA just before committing suicide first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe Achieves Historic Lithium Breakthrough with Africa’s First Battery-Grade Lithium Sulphate
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe has reached a significant milestone in its drive to move beyond raw mineral exports after successfully producing Africa’s first battery-grade lithium sulphate with a purity of at least 99.5%, marking a major step towards building an integrated electric vehicle (EV) battery materials industry. Mines and Mining Development Minister Winston Chitando Kambamura was [...] The post Zimbabwe Achieves Historic Lithium Breakthrough with Africa’s First Battery-Grade Lithium Sulphate appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Four in Court Over 30-Tonne Tanzania Clothing Smuggling Plot @263Chat
item.description: By Judith Nyuke Four men arrested by the Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (ZACC) appeared in court on Thursday, facing charges of smuggling 30 tonnes of clothing from Tanzania. Mallulidi Sultani, Sebastian Mutakwa, Carlton Mutize, and James Mugumbati appeared before Harare Regional Magistrate Jesse Kufa on Thursday. [...] The post Four in Court Over 30-Tonne Tanzania Clothing Smuggling Plot appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Chadzamira commissions clinic, service vehicles in Mwenezi
item.description: George Maponga Masvingo Bureau Masvingo Provincial Affairs and Devolution Minister Ezra Chadzamira has commissioned the newly built Petronela Clinic in Chitanga communal lands in Mwenezi in a major boost for... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: No radiation leak after ‘contamination’ events at Africa’s only nuclear plant, regulator says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa — South Africa’s nuclear regulator said Thursday that no radioactive material leaked into the environment during three recent “contamination” events inside Africa’s only nuclear power station. The incidents involved “elevated airborne radioactive contamination” inside the Koeberg Power Station, on South Africa’s west coast, when there was a loss of power to [...] The post No radiation leak after ‘contamination’ events at Africa’s only nuclear plant, regulator says appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Murowa Diamonds placed under corporate rescue as RioZim debt battle intensifies
item.description: HARARE – RZM Murowa, operator of the Murowa Diamonds mine in Zvishavane, has been placed under voluntary corporate rescue, invoking a statutory moratorium that freezes all pending litigation, asset attachments and debt executions against the diamond miner. The company’s board adopted the corporate rescue resolution on July 6, with proceedings taking effect from July 7 [...] The post Murowa Diamonds placed under corporate rescue as RioZim debt battle intensifies appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mutapa Gold Resources unveils US$212 million expansion plan to lift output
item.description: BINDURA – State-owned Mutapa Gold Resources (MGR) has unveiled a US$212 million expansion programme aimed at sharply increasing gold production over the next three years, as the government pushes to boost bullion output to strengthen the country’s gold-backed currency. MGR chief executive Patrick Museva Shayawabaya said the company was on course to meet its 2026 [...] The post Mutapa Gold Resources unveils US$212 million expansion plan to lift output appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: 9 dead after kombi rolls into Buhera dam as driver loads luggage
item.description: Brake failure suspected in early morning tragedy The post 9 dead after kombi rolls into Buhera dam as driver loads luggage appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: 4 miners confirmed dead after shaft at Zvimba gold mine collapses
item.description: No survivors after overnight mine accident The post 4 miners confirmed dead after shaft at Zvimba gold mine collapses appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Top Government officials, hundreds of mourners gather in Musana for Chipo Reza burial
item.description: Trust Freddy Herald Correspondent HUNDREDS of mourners, including senior Government officials, heads of state agencies, and members of the clergy, have gathered at Chavhunduka Village in Musana, Bindura District, today... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Telecel creditors and owners to vote on a rescue plan @Techzim
item.description: On Friday, 24 July 2026, the people Telecel owes money to, and the people who own it (the government through Mutapa), will meet to vote on a plan to save the company. It’s at the Harare Showgrounds, in the Andy Millar Hall, at 10am. Grant Thornton, who are doing the ‘corporate rescue” process, made the [...] The post Telecel creditors and owners to vote on a rescue plan appeared first on Techzim. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Techzim

item.title: Zimbabwe, China deepen cooperation on liberation war veterans’ welfare @bulawayo24
item.description: BEIJING – Zimbabwe and China have agreed to strengthen cooperation in veterans’ affairs following high‐level discussions between the Minister of Veterans of the Liberation Struggle Affairs, Senator Mo... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Maoneke calls for integrated government systems to improve service delivery @bulawayo24
item.description: VICTORIA FALLS - Chairperson of the Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Defence, Home Affairs, Security Services and War Veterans Affairs, Exevia Moses Maoneke, has urged Government to accelerate the... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: More than 6,100 babies, 563 mothers died in Zimbabwe during 2025 @nehandaradio
item.description: Zimbabwe recorded 6,177 neonatal deaths and 563 maternal deaths in 2025, with Health Minister Douglas Mombeshora citing birth asphyxia, infections and postpartum haemorrhage as the leading causes. The post More than 6,100 babies, 563 mothers died in Zimbabwe during 2025 appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio
